title: বাংলাদেশ ব্যাংক আইসিটি নিরাপত্তা নির্দেশিকা (সংস্করণ ৪.০)
description: The Bangladesh Bank ICT Security Guideline Version 4.0 establishes the mandatory information and communication technology security requirements applicable to all scheduled banks and financial institutions under the supervision of Bangladesh Bank. The Guideline sets out governance, risk management, technical control, and operational security requirements designed to protect the integrity, confidentiality, and availability of financial information systems and to ensure the resilience of the financial sector against cybersecurity threats. It is issued under the authority of the Bangladesh Bank Order 1972 and the Bank Company Act 1991 and is binding on all regulated entities.

provisions.title: Section 1. Scope and Application
provisions.content: The Bangladesh Bank ICT Security Guideline Version 4.0 applies to all scheduled banks and financial institutions regulated by Bangladesh Bank, including state-owned commercial banks, private commercial banks, specialised banks, and non-bank financial institutions. The Guideline applies to all information and communication technology systems used by regulated entities, including core banking systems, digital banking platforms, payment systems, and supporting infrastructure. Regulated entities shall implement all requirements of this Guideline within the timeframes specified and shall maintain ongoing compliance. Bangladesh Bank shall conduct periodic assessments of regulated entities' compliance with this Guideline through on-site and off-site supervision activities. Non-compliance with this Guideline shall be subject to regulatory action under applicable banking law.

provisions.title: Section 2. ICT Governance Framework
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall establish and maintain an ICT governance framework ensuring that ICT risks are identified, assessed, and managed in a manner commensurate with the entity's risk profile and business objectives. ICT governance framework requirements include: Board of Directors responsibility for oversight of ICT risk management; appointment of a Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) with appropriate seniority, authority, and resources; establishment of an ICT Security Committee providing management-level oversight; development and maintenance of an ICT Security Policy approved by the Board; annual review of ICT governance arrangements; and reporting of ICT risk matters to the Board on a regular basis. The CISO shall have direct reporting access to the Board and shall not be subordinate to the Chief Information Officer in matters of cybersecurity policy. ICT governance arrangements shall be documented and shall be reviewed in ICT security assessments.

provisions.title: Section 3. ICT Risk Management
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement a risk-based approach to ICT security, identifying and assessing ICT risks and implementing controls proportionate to identified risks. ICT risk management requirements include: maintaining an ICT risk register documenting identified risks, their likelihood and potential impact, and applicable controls; conducting annual ICT risk assessments, including assessment of emerging threats; integrating ICT risk management with enterprise-wide risk management frameworks; establishing risk appetite statements for ICT risks approved by the Board; monitoring key ICT risk indicators on an ongoing basis; and reporting ICT risk matters to the Risk Committee and Board on a regular basis. Regulated entities shall classify their information assets according to sensitivity and shall apply security controls commensurate with classification. Risk assessment methodologies shall be documented and applied consistently.

provisions.title: Section 4. Network Security
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement comprehensive network security controls protecting their IT infrastructure from unauthorised access, intrusion, and interference. Network security requirements include: implementation of network segmentation separating critical systems, payment networks, and internet-facing systems; deployment of firewall and intrusion detection/prevention systems at network perimeters; implementation of secure remote access mechanisms using multi-factor authentication and encrypted tunnels; monitoring of network traffic for anomalous activity; maintenance of network security architecture documentation; regular review of firewall rules and network access controls; and network penetration testing at intervals specified in this Guideline. Regulated entities shall implement controls preventing the connection of unauthorised devices to their networks. Wireless networks used for business purposes shall implement enterprise-grade security controls.

provisions.title: Section 5. Access Control
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement robust access control mechanisms ensuring that access to information systems and data is limited to authorised persons and is consistent with business need. Access control requirements include: implementation of the principle of least privilege limiting access rights to those necessary for job functions; formal access management processes for user provisioning, modification, and de-provisioning; multi-factor authentication for access to critical systems and privileged accounts; periodic review of user access rights, at least annually; privileged access management controls for system administrator accounts; segregation of duties in critical processes; monitoring of privileged access activities; and immediate revocation of access rights upon employee departure. Regulated entities shall maintain access logs enabling the review of access events for audit and investigation purposes. Default system accounts and passwords shall be changed before deployment.

provisions.title: Section 6. Data Security and Encryption
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement data security controls protecting the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive financial and customer data in storage and in transit. Data security requirements include: encryption of sensitive data at rest using approved encryption algorithms and key lengths; encryption of all data transmitted over public networks using current TLS standards; implementation of data loss prevention controls for sensitive data categories; classification of data assets according to sensitivity; implementation of access controls for sensitive data categories; secure deletion of data no longer required for business or legal purposes; and protection of data on portable devices and removable media. Regulated entities shall not store full payment card details unless specifically authorised by Bangladesh Bank and compliant with applicable payment card industry security standards. Encryption key management shall be implemented in accordance with approved key management standards.

provisions.title: Section 7. Digital Banking and Channel Security
provisions.content: Regulated entities offering digital banking services, including internet banking, mobile banking, and mobile financial services, shall implement security controls protecting the integrity of digital channels and the security of customer transactions. Digital channel security requirements include: strong customer authentication for digital banking access, including multi-factor authentication for high-risk transactions; implementation of transaction monitoring systems detecting anomalous patterns; real-time transaction alerts to customers; secure session management preventing session hijacking; end-to-end encryption of transaction data; protection of digital banking applications against common vulnerabilities; and clear procedures for reporting and responding to digital banking fraud. Regulated entities shall conduct regular security testing of digital banking applications, including mobile applications. Digital banking fraud incidents shall be reported to Bangladesh Bank in accordance with applicable incident reporting requirements.

provisions.title: Section 8. Third-Party and Outsourcing Risk Management
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement controls managing the cybersecurity risks arising from third-party service providers and outsourced ICT functions. Third-party and outsourcing risk management requirements include: due diligence assessment of third-party service providers before engagement; contractual requirements for third-party ICT security, including security standards, incident reporting, and audit rights; ongoing monitoring of third-party security performance; assessment of supply chain risks for critical ICT components and services; Bangladesh Bank notification and approval requirements for material ICT outsourcing arrangements; and incident response coordination with third parties. Regulated entities shall maintain a register of material third-party ICT relationships and shall assess third-party risks as part of their annual ICT risk assessment. Critical ICT outsourcing arrangements shall be subject to Bangladesh Bank's approval in accordance with applicable outsourcing guidelines.

provisions.title: Section 9. Incident Detection and Response
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement capabilities for the timely detection of cybersecurity incidents and the effective management and resolution of incidents. Incident detection and response requirements include: deployment of security information and event management (SIEM) or equivalent monitoring capabilities; documented incident response plans covering different incident categories and severity levels; defined roles and responsibilities for incident response; incident classification and escalation procedures; coordination procedures with Bangladesh Bank, the national CIRT, and law enforcement; post-incident review and reporting; and regular testing of incident response plans through tabletop exercises or simulations. Regulated entities shall report significant cybersecurity incidents to Bangladesh Bank within the timeframes specified in applicable reporting guidelines. Incident response plans shall be reviewed and updated annually.

provisions.title: Section 10. Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall maintain business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities ensuring the continued availability of critical banking services in the event of a major ICT disruption. Business continuity and disaster recovery requirements include: documented Business Continuity Plans (BCP) and Disaster Recovery Plans (DRP) covering ICT systems and services; Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) for critical systems commensurate with business requirements and Bangladesh Bank standards; operational secondary data centres meeting specified geographic separation requirements; regular backup procedures with offsite storage; regular testing of BCP and DRP, including full failover tests at intervals specified by Bangladesh Bank; and integration of ICT business continuity with overall business continuity management. Regulated entities shall submit their BCP and DRP to Bangladesh Bank for review upon request and shall report on the results of continuity tests.

provisions.title: Section 11. Security Testing and Vulnerability Management
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement a comprehensive security testing and vulnerability management programme to identify and remediate security vulnerabilities in their ICT systems. Security testing and vulnerability management requirements include: regular vulnerability scanning of all internet-facing systems at minimum quarterly intervals; annual penetration testing of critical systems by qualified independent testers; application security testing for all new and significantly modified banking applications; patch management processes ensuring timely application of security patches; tracking and remediation of identified vulnerabilities within defined timeframes based on severity; vulnerability disclosure procedures; and management of security findings through a risk-based remediation process. Critical vulnerabilities shall be remediated within timeframes specified by Bangladesh Bank. Security testing results shall be reviewed by the CISO and reported to the ICT Security Committee.

provisions.title: Section 12. Physical Security
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement physical security controls protecting ICT infrastructure, data centres, and sensitive work areas from unauthorised physical access. Physical security requirements include: access controls for data centres and server rooms limiting entry to authorised personnel; environmental controls including power protection, temperature regulation, and fire suppression; physical security monitoring through CCTV and access logging; clear desk and clear screen policies for sensitive work areas; secure disposal of ICT equipment and storage media; and physical security requirements for third-party data centre facilities used by regulated entities. Data centres shall be located in facilities meeting standards for physical security, environmental resilience, and power availability specified by Bangladesh Bank. Physical access to critical systems shall be logged and regularly reviewed.

provisions.title: Section 13. ICT Security Awareness and Training
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall implement a comprehensive ICT security awareness and training programme ensuring that all employees understand their security responsibilities and are equipped to recognise and respond to security threats. ICT security awareness and training requirements include: mandatory annual ICT security awareness training for all employees; role-specific ICT security training for employees with security-sensitive functions including ICT staff, system administrators, and customer-facing staff; regular phishing simulation exercises measuring employee awareness; ICT security training as part of the induction programme for new employees; specialised training for the CISO and ICT security staff; and board-level ICT security education for members of the Board of Directors. Training effectiveness shall be measured and reported to the ICT Security Committee. Awareness programmes shall address the social engineering and phishing threats most commonly targeting financial institutions.

provisions.title: Section 14. Regulatory Reporting and Compliance
provisions.content: Regulated entities shall maintain compliance with this Guideline on a continuous basis and shall report on their ICT security posture to Bangladesh Bank in accordance with applicable reporting requirements. Regulatory reporting requirements include: annual ICT security self-assessment against this Guideline submitted to Bangladesh Bank; immediate reporting of significant cybersecurity incidents, including data breaches, ransomware attacks, and fraud events, within specified timeframes; quarterly reporting of key ICT risk indicators; reporting of material outsourcing arrangements; and provision of information and access to ICT systems for Bangladesh Bank on-site inspections. Bangladesh Bank shall conduct periodic on-site assessments of regulated entities' ICT security arrangements. Non-compliant entities shall be required to submit remediation plans with defined timelines and shall be subject to regulatory escalation for persistent non-compliance.

provisions.title: Section 15. Review and Updates
provisions.content: Bangladesh Bank shall review the ICT Security Guideline periodically to ensure that it remains current with the evolving threat environment, emerging technologies, and international standards and best practices. The review cycle shall be at a minimum every two years, with interim updates issued as required to address material changes in the threat environment or regulatory requirements. Regulated entities shall be consulted in the review process through the Banking Association and direct engagement with major entities. Updated guidelines shall be communicated to regulated entities with a clear implementation timeline allowing adequate preparation. Bangladesh Bank shall issue implementation guidance, FAQs, and sector briefings to support regulated entities in implementing Guideline requirements. Regulated entities may request clarification of Guideline requirements from the Bangladesh Bank ICT and Financial Intelligence Department.

definitions.definition: Any scheduled bank or financial institution supervised by Bangladesh Bank and therefore required to comply with the ICT Security Guideline.
Chief Information Security Officer, the senior executive responsible for ICT security governance within a regulated entity.
Recovery Time Objective and Recovery Point Objective — the maximum tolerable downtime and data loss for a system in the event of a disruption.
Security Information and Event Management system — technology enabling real-time monitoring, correlation, and alerting for security events.
The principle that users and systems should be granted access rights limited to those necessary for their specific job functions or operational requirements.
